caption: TSIG
description: The TSIG (Transaction Signature) object represents a TSIG resource record as defined in RFC 2845. TSIG uses a shared secret key and one-way hash function to authenticate DNS messages. Per RFC 2845, at most one TSIG record is permitted per DNS message.
name: tsig

algorithm.description: The HMAC algorithm used to compute the MAC. For example: hmac-sha256.

error_id.caption: TSIG Error ID
error_id.description: The normalized TSIG-specific error code as defined in RFC 2845 and RFC 6895. This is independent of the DNS message header RCODE.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The TSIG error code is not listed. See the error attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

error.caption: TSIG Error
error.description: The TSIG error, normalized to the caption of the error_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

key_name.description: The name of the shared secret key used to sign the DNS message. For example: my-tsig-key.example.com.

references.description: RFC 2845 — Secret Key Transaction Authentication for DNS (TSIG).
